steps.title: Identify the preceding claim
steps.content: The sentence immediately before the underlined text states that Davis and Evans “felt no desire to please listeners expecting an exact duplication of the opera.” This claim sets up what the next sentence will illustrate.

steps.title: Match the function to the choices
steps.content: Specific examples immediately following a general claim function to support it. Here, the departures—omitting the aria and making brief gestures—exemplify how Davis and Evans departed from the original, supporting the claim. The other choices misidentify the sentence's role: B incorrectly attributes benefit to it, while C and D describe entirely different functions. That matches choice A — A is correct.
